status_detail: v3 SSE envelope NOT being delivered. Engine still doing TCP RST. POST /api/streamingSearch returned errorText=net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED, canceled=false. No Network.responseReceived ever fired. 0 headers, 0 body. UI: you.com generic catch-all error '🤖 Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again later. Error ID: cd259712-...'. Cleaner bubble than #632's raw React TypeError, but functionally identical -- warning text never reaches user. Same outcome as #632 (substitution not happening). KEY FORENSIC: in a single observation window 9 simultaneous you.com API requests all failed with net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED -- entire chat surface torn down at TCP layer, broader than just streamingSearch. Substitution is either: (a) not registered (template id=80 not actually loaded), (b) not URL-pattern-matching POST /api/streamingSearch, (c) failing to write headers on the negotiated HTTP version → falling through to TCP close.
request_status: 0 (no HTTP response -- Network.loadingFailed with errorText=net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED, canceled=false; no Network.responseReceived ever fired)
generic_error_text: 🤖 Sorry, something went wrong. Please try again later. ... Error ID: cd259712-...
generic_error_class_change: you.com NATIVE catch-all error UI (cleaner than #632's raw React TypeError) -- but functionally identical, warning never reaches user
intercept_fired: yes (TCP RST is engine intercept, but wrong substitution method)
broader_chat_surface_failure: 9 simultaneous you.com API requests all failed with net::ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED in single observation window -- entire chat surface torn down at TCP layer
progress_vs_632: SAME -- still TCP-level connection close before headers, still 0 bytes body, still generic error UI. Only cosmetic difference: cleaner native error bubble vs React TypeError. Warning text never reaches user.
breakthrough_status: not achieved -- engine v3 SSE envelope (template id=80, 200 OK + text/event-stream + youChatToken/done events) is NOT being delivered to you.com client
root_cause_hypothesis: Substitution is either: (a) template id=80 not registered or active, (b) URL pattern doesn't match POST /api/streamingSearch (pattern may need POST method explicitly), (c) failing to write headers on negotiated HTTP version → engine falls through to TCP close. Most likely (a) given identical wire behavior to #632.
recommendation_for_dev: Engine team: verify template id=80 (you_sse_v3) registration for service=you. Check DB row exists, response_type matches, URL pattern includes POST method.
Confirm reload_services completed successfully after adding template id=80.
Engine log: grep [APF:you_diag] in /var/log/etap.log for the streamingSearch request between 16:14-16:31 KST 2026-04-28. Did intercept fire? If yes, did SSE generator throw before writing headers?
Compare with gemini3 B36 (which DID emit response correctly) for what makes engine SUCCESS-write SSE vs TCP-close.
After fix, expect: 200 OK + Content-Type text/event-stream + youChatToken event + done event → warning renders in you.com chat bubble.
side_note_methodology: Subagent struggled with you.com persistent login modal (windows-mcp UI clicks couldn't dismiss; F12 didn't reach DevTools through focus-stealing dialog). Pivoted to driving Chrome via --remote-debugging-port=9222 + Python CDP driver (cdp_driver.py) that injects fetch/XHR/EventSource interceptor into the page, programmatically types prompt, dispatches Enter via Input.dispatchKeyEvent. This bypasses login modal (UI overlay only; chat input still works for anonymous turn). Recommend reusing this driver for future you.com checks. Also captured raw CDP event chain at cdp_events_streamingSearch.json.
errors_observed: 9 simultaneous you.com API requests all failed with ERR_CONNECTION_CLOSED
you.com generic error UI: 'Sorry, something went wrong'
notes: Cycle 95 WSA v2 you SSE envelope v3 verify. FAIL = template id=80 not actually substituting; engine still TCP-RST. Subagent 17min exceeded 8min hard timeout due to login-modal struggle, but pivot to CDP driver produced clean capture and reusable artifact.
